key_insight: Neural network depth is adapted by treating training as an optimal control problem and inserting new layers where a posteriori error is estimated to be largest.
engineering_relevance: This provides a principled, FEM-inspired way to refine surrogate models for scientific computing problems when accuracy needs to improve without blindly increasing network size.
method: Formulate NN training as a continuous-time optimal control problem with layer-wise weights and biases.
Derive dual-weighted a posteriori error bounds that decompose approximation error across depth intervals.
Insert new layers at locations of maximum estimated error to guide architecture refinement.
evidence_signal: The method was tested on scientific datasets, including learning the observable-to-parameter map for the Navier-Stokes equation, and the abstract reports improved generalization versus existing adaptation methods.
watch_item: The approach adds estimator and optimization complexity, so its effectiveness depends on accurate error localization and a stable implementation of the depth-adaptation loop.
paper_title: An optimal control approach for neural network architecture adaptation with a posteriori error estimation
